diffs.patch:
Index: content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx
===================================================================
--- content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx	
+++ content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx	
@@ -0,0 +1,55 @@
+---
+title: Editable Chip
+description: An inline-editable chip component with animated transitions between display and edit modes.
+---
+
+import { EditableChipPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo";
+
+<ComponentPreview name="editable-chip">
+  <EditableChipPreview />
+</ComponentPreview>
+
+## Installation
+
+If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
+
+<CodeBlockCommand
+  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
+  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
+  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
+  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
+/>
+
+Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
+
+<CodeBlockCommand
+  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
+  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
+  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
+  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
+/>
+
+## Usage
+
+After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
+
+```tsx
+import { EditableChip } from "@/components/ui/editable-chip";
+
+export function MyComponent() {
+  return (
+    <EditableChip
+      defaultLabel="Watchlist"
+      onChange={(value) => console.log(value)}
+    />
+  );
+}
+```
+
+## Props
+
+| Prop              | Type                      | Default       | Description                               |
+| ----------------- | ------------------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
+| `defaultLabel`    | `string`                  | `"Watchlist"` | Initial text displayed in the chip        |
+| `showThemeToggle` | `boolean`                 | -             | Whether to show a theme toggle (reserved) |
+| `onChange`        | `(value: string) => void` | -             | Callback fired when the label is saved    |

Index: registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo.tsx =================================================================== --- registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo.tsx +++ registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo.tsx @@ -0,0 +1,9 @@ +"use client"; + +import { EditableChip } from "./editable-chip"; + +export const EditableChipPreview = () => ( +
+ +
+);

state.output:
<skill_content name="launch-shadcn-registry">
# Skill: launch-shadcn-registry

# Launch shadcn Registry

Orchestrate listing and announcing a custom shadcn/ui registry across official and community directories, then draft platform-specific social posts.

## Before You Start

Confirm the user wants a **full launch** or a **subset** (e.g. official directory only, or social drafts only). Default to the full workflow unless they narrow scope.

Collect or infer a **registry profile**. If missing fields, ask before generating artifacts. Use [templates/registry-profile.example.json](templates/registry-profile.example.json) as the shape; validate against [templates/registry-profile.schema.json](templates/registry-profile.schema.json).

Required profile fields:

| Field | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `scope` | Registry scope, e.g. `@ogimagecn` |
| `name` | Display name |
| `homepage` | Marketing/docs URL |
| `registryBaseUrl` | Base URL for JSON files (no trailing slash) |
| `componentUrlPattern` | URL pattern with `{name}` placeholder, e.g. `https://example.com/r/{name}.json` |
| `description` | One sentence (≤160 chars) for directories |
| `descriptionLong` | 2–3 sentences for awesome lists and social |
| `githubUrl` | Source repo |
| `githubUsername` | For avatar URLs and filenames |
| `distribution` | `open-source` or `premium` |
| `categories` | Tags for shadcntemplates / awesome lists |
| `installExample` | e.g. `npx shadcn@latest add @scope/component-name` |
| `logo` | Inline SVG string for official directory (optional but recommended) |
| `registryIndexPath` | Path to registry index relative to `registryBaseUrl`, defaults to `/registry.json` (optional) |
| `sampleComponents` | Array of component slugs to validate during preflight, e.g. `["og-image"]` (optional) |
| `features` | Bullet points for shadcntemplates and social posts (optional) |

## Workflow Overview

Copy this checklist and track progress:

```
Launch Progress:
- [ ] Phase 1: Preflight validation
- [ ] Phase 2: Generate directory artifacts
- [ ] Phase 3: Open GitHub PRs (user approval per PR)
- [ ] Phase 4: Draft social posts
- [ ] Phase 5: Post-launch follow-up
```

## Phase 1: Preflight

Read [references/preflight.md](references/preflight.md) and run validation before generating any PRs.

```bash
bash scripts/validate-registry.sh <registryBaseUrl> [sample-component-name]
```

Fix all blockers before continuing. Common failures:

- `registry.json` not public or wrong path
- Component JSON 404
- Duplicate `@scope` in official directory
- `description` too long or marketing-heavy for official listing

Fetch the official directory to check duplicates:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/main/apps/v4/registry/directory.json | grep -i "<scope>"
```

## Phase 2: Generate Artifacts

From the registry profile, generate all submission files. Read the relevant reference **only for targets the user wants**:

| Target | Reference | Output |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Official shadcn-ui/ui | [references/shadcn-ui-official.md](references/shadcn-ui-official.md) | JSON entry + PR body |
| registry.directory | [references/registry-directory.md](references/registry-directory.md) | JSON entry + PR body |
| shadcntemplates | [references/shadcntemplates.md](references/shadcntemplates.md) | `content/{author}-{name}.md` |
| birobirobiro awesome | [references/awesome-birobirobiro.md](references/awesome-birobirobiro.md) | README table row |
| bytefer awesome | [references/awesome-bytefer.md](references/awesome-bytefer.md) | README table row |

Present artifacts grouped by repo. Include:

1. Exact file path to create or edit
2. Full content to add (not diffs alone)
3. Suggested PR title and body

### PR title convention

Use `feat(registry): add <scope>` for official shadcn-ui/ui (matches [PR #10896](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/pull/10896)). Use `docs: add <name> to <section>` for awesome lists and `feat: add <name> registry` for registry.directory.

## Phase 3: Open GitHub PRs

Use `gh` for all GitHub operations. **Ask the user before creating each PR** unless they explicitly asked to open all PRs.

Standard fork-and-PR flow per repo:

```bash
# Example: official shadcn-ui/ui
gh repo fork shadcn-ui/ui --clone=false
git clone https://github.com/<user>/ui.git /tmp/ui-registry-pr
cd /tmp/ui-registry-pr
git remote add upstream https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui.git
git fetch upstream && git checkout -b feat/<scope>-directory upstream/main
# edit apps/v4/registry/directory.json — append entry at end of array
git add apps/v4/registry/directory.json
git commit -m "feat(registry): add <scope>"
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --repo shadcn-ui/ui --title "feat(registry): add <scope>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
Adds <scope> to the community registry directory.

- **Registry URL**: `<componentUrlPattern>`
- **Homepage**: <homepage>
- **Description**: <description>

## Test plan
- [ ] `curl <registryBaseUrl>/registry.json` returns valid JSON
- [ ] `npx shadcn@latest add <installExample>` installs successfully
EOF
)"
```

**Recommended PR order:**

1. Official `shadcn-ui/ui` (highest leverage; may take maintainer review)
2. `rbadillap/registry.directory`
3. `shadcnblocks/shadcntemplates`
4. `birobirobiro/awesome-shadcn-ui` and `bytefer/awesome-shadcn-ui` (can be parallel)

Track PR URLs in the launch checklist. Do not draft social posts as "published" until the registry is live and at least one directory listing is merged or submitted.

## Phase 4: Social Drafts

Read platform references and produce **ready-to-paste drafts** (user posts manually):

| Platform | Reference |
|----------|-----------|
| Reddit r/shadcn | [references/social/reddit.md](references/social/reddit.md) |
| X | [references/social/x.md](references/social/x.md) |
| Dev.to | [references/social/devto.md](references/social/devto.md) |
| Hacker News | [references/social/hackernews.md](references/social/hackernews.md) |

Tailor each draft from the registry profile. Include the install command, homepage, and GitHub link. Avoid hype; lead with the concrete problem the registry solves.

**Timing:** Draft social posts after preflight passes. Suggest posting after the official directory PR is merged (or at minimum after `registry.json` is live).

## Phase 5: Post-Launch

After PRs are open or merged:

1. Share PR links with the user in a summary table (repo, PR URL, status)
2. Remind user to respond to review comments within 48h
3. After official merge, suggest updating social drafts to mention "now listed in the shadcn registry directory"
4. If a PR is rejected, read maintainer feedback and adjust description or registry format — do not resubmit unchanged

## Rules

- Never submit to directories before preflight passes
- Never claim listings are live until PRs are merged
- Never auto-post to Reddit, X, Dev.to, or HN — drafts only
- Keep official `directory.json` entry at the **end** of the array (append only)
- One registry profile drives all artifacts — do not invent different descriptions per platform without user approval
- For premium registries on shadcntemplates, note the one-free / $100 multi-premium policy; do not promise approval

## Example

**User:** "Launch @ogimagecn — https://ogimagecn.vercel.app"

**Agent:**

1. Build profile from site + `curl https://ogimagecn.vercel.app/r/registry.json`
2. Run `validate-registry.sh https://ogimagecn.vercel.app og-image`
3. Generate JSON for official directory, registry.directory, shadcntemplates markdown, awesome list rows
4. Ask: "Open PRs to all 5 repos?"
5. On approval, create PRs via `gh`
6. Deliver Reddit, X, Dev.to, and HN drafts

## Additional Resources

- Registry profile schema: [templates/registry-profile.schema.json](templates/registry-profile.schema.json)
- Example profile: [templates/registry-profile.example.json](templates/registry-profile.example.json)
- Validation script: [scripts/validate-registry.sh](scripts/validate-registry.sh)

Base directory for this skill: /home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/shadcntemplates.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/shadcn-ui-official.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/registry-directory.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/preflight.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/awesome-bytefer.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/awesome-birobirobiro.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/social/x.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/social/reddit.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/social/hackernews.md</file>
<file>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/.agents/skills/launch-shadcn-registry/references/social/devto.md</file>
</skill_files>
</skill_content>

parts.text:
The user wants me to update docs and demo for a new component `@registry/new-york/editable-chip.tsx` and then run `pnpm fix`. This is not about launching a registry - it's about updating documentation and demo files for a newly added component within an existing registry project.

Let me explore the codebase to understand:
1. How docs are structured
2. How demos are structured
3. What patterns exist for other components

input.prompt:
Explore the /home/himmel/personal/prsui codebase to understand:
1. How documentation pages are structured (look for docs/, content/, app/docs/ patterns)
2. How demo pages/components are structured
3. Find examples of existing component docs and demos to understand the pattern
4. Check if there's already an editable-chip doc or demo file
5. Look at the registry structure to understand how components are registered

Return:
- The directory structure for docs and demos
- Examples of existing component doc files (full content of 1-2 examples)
- Examples of existing demo files (full content of 1-2 examples)
- The registry index file content (registry.json or similar)
- Any existing editable-chip references

state.output:
Here are the complete findings from the codebase exploration. --- ## 1. Directory Structure for Docs and Demos ``` /home/himmel/personal/prsui/ ├── content/ │ └── docs/ │ ├── meta.json # Root nav: pages ["(root)", "components"] │ ├── (root)/ │ │ ├── index.mdx # Homepage │ │ └── meta.json │ └── components/ │ ├── meta.json # Nav order: ["index","smooth-input","split-action","tooltip","accordion","typewriter","link"] │ ├── index.mdx # listing │ ├── accordion.mdx │ ├── link.mdx │ ├── smooth-input.mdx │ ├── split-action.mdx │ ├── tooltip.mdx │ └── typewriter.mdx │ ├── registry/ │ └── new-york/ │ ├── accordion.tsx # Component source │ ├── accordion-demo.tsx # Demo/preview for docs │ ├── editable-chip.tsx # <-- EXISTS (no demo, no doc) │ ├── link.tsx │ ├── link-demo.tsx │ ├── smooth-input.tsx # (no demo file) │ ├── split-action.tsx │ ├── split-action-demo.tsx │ ├── tooltip.tsx │ ├── tooltip-demo.tsx │ ├── typewriter.tsx │ └── typewriter-demo.tsx │ ├── registry.json # Source of truth for shadcn registry ├── public/r/registry.json # Built/deployed copy (identical) └── lib/registry.ts # Helper: getRegistryUiSourceCandidates() ``` **Key pattern**: No `examples/` directory exists. Demos live as `-demo.tsx` files directly in `registry/new-york/` alongside the component source. --- ## 2. Component Doc File Examples (full content) ### Example 1: `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/accordion.mdx` ```mdx --- title: Accordion description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes. --- import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/accordion-demo"; ## Installation If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: Otherwise, use the full registry URL: ## Usage After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: // code block with import + usage example ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | ... | ... | ... | ... | ``` ### Example 2: `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/typewriter.mdx` Same structure: frontmatter, import demo, ``, Installation (short + URL), Usage code block, Props table. **The consistent doc MDX pattern is:** 1. YAML frontmatter with `title` and `description` 2. Import the demo component from `@/registry/new-york/-demo` 3. `` 4. `## Installation` with two `` blocks (short + URL) 5. `## Usage` with a code block showing import and basic usage 6. `## Props` as a markdown table --- ## 3. Demo File Examples (full content) ### Example 1: `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/accordion-demo.tsx` ```tsx "use client"; import { BookOpen, Bug, Info, LayoutGrid, Space, SunMoon } from "lucide-react"; import { Accordion } from "./accordion"; const items = [ /* ... */ ]; export const BouncyAccordionPreview = function BouncyAccordionPreview() { return (
); }; ``` ### Example 2: `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/tooltip-demo.tsx` ```tsx "use client"; import { Heart, Settings, Share, Trash2 } from "lucide-react"; import { Tooltip } from "./tooltip"; export const TooltipPreview = () => (
{/* ... more tooltips */}

Hover or focus each button. Content fades and un-blurs in.

); ``` **The consistent demo pattern is:** 1. `"use client"` directive at top 2. Import the component from the sibling file (`./accordion`, `./tooltip`, etc.) 3. Export a named component (e.g., `AccordionPreview`, `BouncyAccordionPreview`, `TooltipPreview`) 4. Simple wrapper div with Tailwind layout classes 5. Renders the component with realistic example data/props --- ## 4. Registry Structure ### `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry.json` (root, source of truth) Each entry follows this schema: ```json { "name": "", "type": "registry:ui", "title": "", "description": "...", "files": [ { "path": "registry/new-york/<name>.tsx", "type": "registry:ui", "target": "components/ui/<name>.tsx" } ], "dependencies": ["motion", "lucide-react", ...] } ``` **Key observations:** - The registry has 6 registered components: `smooth-input`, `split-action`, `tooltip`, `accordion`, `typewriter`, `link` - `tooltip` and `accordion` also register lib files (`lib/ease.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-hover-capable.ts`) - Demo files are NOT listed in the registry -- only the main component file is registered - The `lib/registry.ts` helper resolves sources from `registry/new-york/<name>.tsx` --- ## 5. Editable-Chip Status **Existing files found:** - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/editable-chip.tsx` -- the component source exists (120 lines, uses `motion/react`, `react-icons/bi`, `lucide-react`) **What is MISSING (not yet created):** - No `registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo.tsx` demo file - No `content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx` doc page - No entry in `registry.json` for `editable-chip` - No entry in `content/docs/components/meta.json` pages list - No entry in `public/r/registry.json` In short: the component source code is in place, but it has not been wired into the docs, the demo system, or the registry yet. </task_result> </task>

17:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
18:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
19:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
20:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
21: />
22: 
23: Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
24: 
25: <CodeBlockCommand
26:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
27:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
28:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
29:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
30: />
31: 
32: ## Usage
33: 
34: After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
35: 
36: ```tsx
37: import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion";
38: 
39: const items: AccordionItem[] = [
40:   {
41:     id: "item-1",
42:     title: "First question",
43:     description: "Answer to the first question.",
44:   },
45:   {
46:     id: "item-2",
47:     title: "Second question",
48:     description: "Answer to the second question.",
49:   },
50: ];
51: 
52: export function MyComponent() {
53:   return <Accordion items={items} defaultValue="item-1" />;
54: }
55: ```
56: 
57: ## Props
58: 
59: | Prop            | Type                              | Default | Description                                           |
60: | --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
61: | `items`         | `AccordionItem[]`                 | -       | Array of accordion items                              |
62: | `value`         | `string \| null`                  | -       | Controlled currently open item id                     |
63: | `defaultValue`  | `string \| null`                  | `null`  | Initially open item id                                |
64: | `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | -       | Callback fired when the open item changes             |
65: | `collapsible`   | `boolean`                         | `true`  | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking |
66: | `className`     | `string`                          | -       | Classes applied to the root wrapper                   |
67: | `classNames`    | `AccordionClassNames`             | -       | Override classes for individual sub-elements          |
68: 
69: ### AccordionItem
70: 
71: | Field         | Type        | Description                              |
72: | ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- |
73: | `id`          | `string`    | Unique identifier for the item           |
74: | `title`       | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row         |
75: | `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open   |
76: | `icon`        | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title |
77: | `disabled`    | `boolean`   | Disables the item                        |
78: 
79: ### AccordionClassNames
80: 
81: | Key           | Description                  |
82: | ------------- | ---------------------------- |
83: | `root`        | Wrapper around all items     |
84: | `item`        | Each accordion row container |
85: | `trigger`     | The clickable button row     |
86: | `icon`        | The icon wrapper span        |
87: | `title`       | The title text span          |
88: | `chevron`     | The chevron rotation wrapper |
89: | `content`     | The animated content wrapper |
90: | `description` | The description text div     |

(End of file - total 90 lines)
</content>

---

<ComponentPreview name="accordion">

</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"

display.text:
---
title: Accordion
description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes.
---

import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/accordion-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="accordion">
  <BouncyAccordionPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion";

const items: AccordionItem[] = [
  {
    id: "item-1",
    title: "First question",
    description: "Answer to the first question.",
  },
  {
    id: "item-2",
    title: "Second question",
    description: "Answer to the second question.",
  },
];

export function MyComponent() {
  return <Accordion items={items} defaultValue="item-1" />;
}
```

## Props

| Prop            | Type                              | Default | Description                                           |
| --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `items`         | `AccordionItem[]`                 | -       | Array of accordion items                              |
| `value`         | `string \| null`                  | -       | Controlled currently open item id                     |
| `defaultValue`  | `string \| null`                  | `null`  | Initially open item id                                |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | -       | Callback fired when the open item changes             |
| `collapsible`   | `boolean`                         | `true`  | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking |
| `className`     | `string`                          | -       | Classes applied to the root wrapper                   |
| `classNames`    | `AccordionClassNames`             | -       | Override classes for individual sub-elements          |

### AccordionItem

| Field         | Type        | Description                              |
| ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `id`          | `string`    | Unique identifier for the item           |
| `title`       | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row         |
| `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open   |
| `icon`        | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title |
| `disabled`    | `boolean`   | Disables the item                        |

### AccordionClassNames

| Key           | Description                  |
| ------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `root`        | Wrapper around all items     |
| `item`        | Each accordion row container |
| `trigger`     | The clickable button row     |
| `icon`        | The icon wrapper span        |
| `title`       | The title text span          |
| `chevron`     | The chevron rotation wrapper |
| `content`     | The animated content wrapper |
| `description` | The description text div     |

input.content:
---
title: Editable Chip
description: An inline-editable chip component with animated transitions between display and edit modes.
---

import { EditableChipPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="editable-chip">
  <EditableChipPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { EditableChip } from "@/components/ui/editable-chip";

export function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <EditableChip
      defaultLabel="Watchlist"
      onChange={(value) => console.log(value)}
    />
  );
}
```

## Props

| Prop            | Type                   | Default       | Description                                      |
| --------------- | ---------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `defaultLabel`  | `string`               | `"Watchlist"` | Initial text displayed in the chip               |
| `showThemeToggle` | `boolean`            | -             | Whether to show a theme toggle (reserved)        |
| `onChange`       | `(value: string) => void` | -          | Callback fired when the label is saved           |
